title: Perth 14-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Perth 14-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Perth, Australia: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Rottnest Island day trip and AQWA (Aquarium of Western Australia). Prices from $3407 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Perth, Australia — Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 31-32°C, December-February) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 18°C, June-August). September-November and March-April, for warm but not extreme temperatures and lower rainfall. This itinerary blends Rottnest Island day trip, AQWA (Aquarium of Western Australia), Kings Park and Botanic Garden with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Rottnest Island day trip, AQWA (Aquarium of Western Australia), Kings Park and Botanic Garden
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Perth
itinerary.description: Land at Perth Airport (PER) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Rottnest Island day trip — Ferry to a car-free island famous for quokka selfies and beaches. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Perth: AQWA (Aquarium of Western Australia) & Pinnacles Desert tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to AQWA (Aquarium of Western Australia) — Aquarium showcasing WA's marine life including sharks. In the afternoon, continue to Pinnacles Desert tour — Day trip to a surreal desert landscape of limestone spires north of Perth. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Perth: Fremantle Prison tour & Kings Park and Botanic Garden
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Fremantle Prison tour — Former colonial-era prison, now a heritage museum. In the afternoon, continue to Kings Park and Botanic Garden — One of the world's largest inner-city parks, with skyline views. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Rottnest Island
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Rottnest Island, a popular side trip from Perth. Return to Perth in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Wave Rock (further afield, near Hyden) — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Perth: Cottesloe Beach & Fremantle Markets area
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Cottesloe Beach — Perth's iconic sunset beach. In the afternoon, continue to Fremantle Markets area — Historic port town with markets, street art, and cafés (market entry itself is free to browse). Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Fremantle
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Fremantle, a popular side trip from Perth. Return to Perth in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Perth: Elizabeth Quay & Rottnest Island day trip
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Elizabeth Quay — Waterfront precinct on the Swan River. In the afternoon, continue to Rottnest Island day trip — Ferry to a car-free island famous for quokka selfies and beaches. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Yanchep National Park — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Swan Valley wine region
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Swan Valley wine region, a popular side trip from Perth. Return to Perth in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Swan Valley wine region

itinerary.title: Perth: AQWA (Aquarium of Western Australia) & Pinnacles Desert tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to AQWA (Aquarium of Western Australia) — Aquarium showcasing WA's marine life including sharks. In the afternoon, continue to Pinnacles Desert tour — Day trip to a surreal desert landscape of limestone spires north of Perth. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Perth: Fremantle Prison tour & Kings Park and Botanic Garden
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Fremantle Prison tour — Former colonial-era prison, now a heritage museum. In the afternoon, continue to Kings Park and Botanic Garden — One of the world's largest inner-city parks, with skyline views. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Fremantle's Bathers Beach — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Cottesloe Beach or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to PER for your departure flight.

travelTips: Take the ferry to Rottnest Island for at least a half-day — renting a bike there is the classic way to see it and spot quokkas.
Use the free CAT bus loops to get around the CBD and Fremantle without a fare.
Watch the sunset at Cottesloe Beach — it's a beloved local ritual.
Book a Pinnacles Desert tour combined with Yanchep National Park for a fuller day trip north of the city.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Strong sun protection year-round — WA's UV index runs high
A light jacket for cooler winter evenings
thingsToDo: Swimming with wild dolphins at Rockingham
Rottnest Island bike ride and quokka spotting
Kings Park playgrounds and picnic areas
thingsToAvoid: Touching or picking up quokkas on Rottnest Island — it's illegal and can result in a fine.
Underestimating WA's sun intensity — sunburn happens quickly even on cloudy days.
Skipping sun protection during outdoor day trips.
topRestaurants: Cicerello's Fremantle — Fish and chips, waterfront ($$)
familyActivities: Rottnest Island bike ride and quokka spotting
Kings Park playgrounds and picnic areas
adventureActivities: Swimming with wild dolphins at Rockingham
localEtiquette: Perth has a relaxed, outdoorsy culture — casual dress is the norm even in many restaurants.
Tipping is not customary, though appreciated for excellent service.
Sun safety is taken seriously — locals routinely apply sunscreen and wear hats.
localTransportGuide: Perth's train and bus network (Transperth) covers the metro area well, and the free CAT bus loops cover the CBD and Fremantle at no cost. Rottnest Island is reached by ferry from Perth, Fremantle, or Hillarys and is entirely car-free once there — bikes are the main way to get around.
bestTimeToVisit: September-November and March-April, for warm but not extreme temperatures and lower rainfall.
weather: Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 31-32°C, December-February) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 18°C, June-August).
visaInformation: All visitors to Australia need a visa or electronic travel authority arranged before departure — an eVisitor or ETA for most Western passport holders, or an e-visitor visa for Indian nationals, all applied for online in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~14.1h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Perth?
faqs.answer: All visitors to Australia need a visa or electronic travel authority arranged before departure — an eVisitor or ETA for most Western passport holders, or an e-visitor visa for Indian nationals, all applied for online in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Perth?
faqs.answer: September-November and March-April, for warm but not extreme temperatures and lower rainfall. Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 31-32°C, December-February) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 18°C, June-August).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Perth safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Perth is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Perth?
faqs.answer: The local currency is AUD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: It also works well for families — highlights include Rottnest Island bike ride and quokka spotting.

reviews.author: Sarah Khan
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Perth is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Elizabeth Quay. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Tom Kim
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Kings Park and Botanic Garden and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
